pr.title: Matching player c  - WIP and non matching.
pr.author: snuffysasa

summary: Closed, unmerged WIP attempt to decompile and name the large player module. The PR replaced much of asm/melee/pl/player.s with a very large src/melee/pl/player.c implementation gated by an explicit `#define NON_MATCHING 1`, expanded src/melee/pl/player.h with a more detailed StaticPlayer layout and many Player_* prototypes, and propagated new Player_* symbol names through many asm callsites. The author stated the DOL would not match until all functions in the file were finished, that NON_MATCHING mode compiled but did not match, and that without it there were unresolved linking issues. The most reusable evidence is the naming pass and struct/header exploration, especially the use of unions/arrays for repeated StaticPlayer fields and the note that struct casting helped match data externs against player.s declarations.
what_changed: Added roughly 8k lines to src/melee/pl/player.c while deleting most of asm/melee/pl/player.s, indicating a broad WIP C translation of the player module rather than a small isolated matching change.
Introduced `#define NON_MATCHING 1` in player.c; the PR body explicitly says this mode compiles but does not match, while disabling it produced linking errors.

Renamed the player slot storage from `lbl_80453080` to `player_slots` and changed uses such as `Player_GetPtrForSlot` and reset/init routines to index `player_slots[slot]`; the PR notes uncertainty over `[4]` versus `[6]` slots.

Expanded `StaticPlayer` in player.h with named fields, arrays, unions, bitfields, and function-pointer storage, including fields such as `player_poses`, `staminas`, `falls[2]`, `kos_by_player[6]`, `team`, `defense_ratio`, `total_coins`, `total_attack_count`, and flag bitfield groups.

Added a large set of Player_* declarations in player.h, covering entity lookup, slot type, character, stocks, coins, damage/HP, KOs, flags, stale move tables, facing direction, model scale, and initialization helpers.

Updated assembly callsites across camera, debug, fighter, character, game, interface, stage, and other asm files from anonymous names like `func_80034110`, `func_8003241C`, and `func_80032330` to semantic names such as `Player_GetEntity`, `Player_GetPlayerSlotType`, and `Player_GetPlayerCharacter`.

Added local experimental structs in player.c, including bitfield-bearing `Unk_Struct`/`Unk_Struct2` and `Unk_Struct_w_Array`, plus externs for player-related string/data labels such as `lbl_803BCDC0`, `lbl_803BCDCC`, and `lbl_803BCDE0`.
smart_moves: Kept the work explicitly marked as WIP/nonmatching instead of presenting it as a final match; the PR body warned that the DOL would not match and that merge timing/squashing should wait.
Used a NON_MATCHING compile path to allow progress to build while preserving the fact that the matching path still had unresolved link or data-declaration problems.
Performed a broad symbol-naming sweep in asm callsites, making later decomp work more searchable and turning repeated calls into recognizable player API usage patterns.

Modeled repeated StaticPlayer regions with unions that provide both named access and indexed access, for example named player position vectors versus `Vec byIndex[4]`, and named stamina fields versus `s16 byIndex[4]`.
Captured uncertain but useful struct/data insights directly in comments, including uncertainty about `player_slots[6]` and the data-label grouping around `lbl_803BCDC0`/`lbl_803BCDE0`.
decomp_lessons: For very large modules, landing a naming/header pass separately from a nonmatching C translation may be easier to review and integrate; this PR combined a huge player.c rewrite with global asm callsite renames and remained unmerged.
When decompiling code that treats the same memory both as individually named fields and as arrays, unions can preserve both source readability and indexing patterns needed for matching.
Data-label shape matters: the author specifically called out a struct-with-or-without-array issue and said struct casting made functions match and externs line up with assembly data declarations.
Before replacing a large asm file, ensure all referenced C symbols and data externs link without relying on an unconditional NON_MATCHING path.

Semantic names such as `Player_GetEntity`, `Player_GetEntityAtIndex`, `Player_GetPlayerSlotType`, `Player_GetPlayerCharacter`, and `Player_GetPlayerId` are high-value because they appear throughout camera, fighter, game, UI, and stage code.
naming_conventions: Anonymous player functions were renamed with a `Player_` module prefix.
Accessor-style functions used `Player_Get...` and mutators used `Player_Set...`, for example `Player_GetEntity`, `Player_SetCoins`, `Player_GetDefenseRatio`, and `Player_SetFacingDirection`.
Unknown-but-address-known functions retained the module prefix plus address suffix, for example `Player_800326CC`, `Player_80031DC8`, and `Player_8003544C`.
Indexed accessors used explicit suffixes like `ByIndex` or `AtIndex`, such as `Player_GetEntityAtIndex`, `Player_SetHPByIndex`, `Player_GetFallsByIndex`, and `Player_UpdateJoystickCountByIndex`.
Global data naming moved from raw labels toward semantic names where possible, notably `lbl_80453080` to `player_slots`, while unresolved data labels remained as `lbl_...` externs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved asm fallback blocks for some functions while adding C versions guarded by `NON_MATCHING`/matching conditionals.
Updated branch-and-link targets in asm files to renamed Player_* symbols without changing instruction bytes, using symbol renames as a cross-module documentation and linking tactic.
Used bitfield structs for byte flags at offsets 0xAC, 0xAD, and 0xAE in StaticPlayer to express individual flag-bit access patterns.
Used struct casting and alternative struct/data declarations to reason about contiguous string/vector data, according to the PR body.
Modeled player state arrays explicitly where assembly indexed repeated fields, including player positions, stamina/HP fields, falls, KOs, joystick counts, and entity arrays.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The author self-identified the PR as progress sharing, not necessarily ready to merge, and noted that future work could squash commits.
The author reported unresolved linking errors when not compiling with `#define NON_MATCHING 1`.
follow_up_queries: Find the later PR that successfully landed matching player.c work and compare which parts of this WIP were reused.
Verify whether `player_slots` should be declared with 4 or 6 entries in the final project state.
Trace final definitions for `lbl_803BCDC0`, `lbl_803BCDCC`, and `lbl_803BCDE0` to see how the struct-with-array data issue was resolved.
Check whether `StaticPlayer` unions for positions, stamina, falls, KOs, joystick counts, and flag bitfields survived in later merged headers.
Search for the final names of `Player_800326CC`, `Player_80031DC8`, `Player_8003544C`, and other address-suffixed Player_* functions.
